text: When you find a bug in your code, the first step is identifying where the problem is. A systematic approach is important: start by checking the most recent changes you made, then test one part of the code at a time. Using debugging tools like breakpoints helps you pause the program and inspect variable values. Another useful strategy is logging, which means adding print statements or log messages to track what your code is doing step by step. This can reveal unexpected behavior. By staying calm and following a clear plan, you can fix errors more quickly and avoid frustration.
